summary: Reviewed the host-controller tweaks against the SPEC and the mandatory review checks. The main risk is false confidence around “unchanged” display markup: the refactor changes both rendered text and likely DOM attributes. Host briefing wiring is mostly present, but a few assumptions around input validity, first-game lookup, and stage labels need correction or verification.
appears_sound: `src/app/host/[sessionId]/[gameId]/game-control.tsx` renders `PreGameBriefing` only when `currentGameState.numbers_called_count === 0`, and hides the old stats/snowball strip during that state.
`src/components/host/pre-game-briefing.tsx` gates the house-rules block behind `isFirstGameOfSession`, matching the intended first-game-only rule behavior.
`src/lib/colour-name.ts` uses a 12-colour palette and returns the literal `Unknown colour` for clearly invalid inputs such as empty strings, shorthand hex, and non-hex text.

findings.description: The display rules refactor is not byte-identical to the old display output. It changes the rule text apostrophe and also routes default rule wrapper spans through `className={cn(...)}`, which can emit an empty `class` attribute where the original had none.
findings.evidence: The original display JSX used `they're` and an unclassed text `<span>`, while `HOUSE_RULES` now stores `they’re` at `src/lib/house-rules.ts:29` and `display-ui.tsx` always passes `className={cn(rule.variant === 'closing' && 'font-bold italic')}` to that wrapper.
findings.why_it_might_be_wrong: A screenshot diff might not visibly catch the empty class attribute, but the text punctuation difference is still a real rendered copy/DOM change.

findings.description: `getColourName` accepts bare six-character hex strings even though the SPEC defines valid input as `#rrggbb`. That means an invalid DB value like `ffffff` would be labelled `White` while the swatch style would not receive a valid CSS hex colour.
findings.evidence: `HEX_RE = /^#?([0-9a-fA-F]{6})$/` makes the leading hash optional, and `src/lib/colour-name.test.ts` explicitly asserts `getColourName('ffffff') === 'White'`.
findings.why_it_might_be_wrong: The implementation may intentionally be more tolerant than the SPEC, but that assumption is not stated in the SPEC and contradicts the invalid-input rule.

findings.description: The briefing renders raw `stage_sequence` values as labels, which may show machine keys such as `two_lines` instead of host-readable labels. Existing display code defines `formatStageLabel`, which suggests the repo normally formats stage strings before presentation.
findings.evidence: `PreGameBriefing` prints `Stage {i + 1}: {stage}` directly, while `display-ui.tsx` includes a `formatStageLabel` helper that replaces underscores and title-cases stage strings.
findings.why_it_might_be_wrong: The SPEC claims the values are already display labels, so this is only a defect if the actual DB/admin flow stores key-like values.
findings.what_would_confirm: Inspect the games type/admin form seed values or a representative `stage_sequence` row to see whether values are display labels or keys.

findings.description: The first-game flag assumes the URL `sessionId` matches the loaded `gameId` and silently treats lookup failure as “not first game.” A mismatched route or transient query failure would suppress the house-rules block for the actual first game instead of failing loudly or redirecting.
findings.evidence: The page fetches `game` by `.eq('id', gameId)` only, then queries the first game with `.eq('session_id', sessionId)` and computes `!!firstGame && game.game_index === firstGame.game_index` without checking `error`.
findings.why_it_might_be_wrong: Normal navigation may always generate matching route params, and the prior successful game/session queries make a transient failure uncommon.
findings.what_would_confirm: Verify route generation guarantees matching params, or add a test/guard requiring `game.session_id === sessionId` and handling `firstGame` query errors.

empty_categories: No server-action, RLS, or data-model changes were introduced by the host briefing implementation.
No evidence that the call-delay enforcement was moved client-side; the change only removes the host-facing hint.
No issue found with the main spacing diet class replacements listed in the SPEC table; the specified class values are present in `game-control.tsx`.
